shots.title: Extend SlopeStore interface + PG hardening
shots.notes: Added StoreStats type and getSchemaVersion()/getStats() to SlopeStore interface. SQLite: renamed sync method to private getSchemaVersionSync(), added async wrapper and compound subquery for getStats(). PG: implemented both methods with project_id-scoped queries, added connection string validation (URL parsing) and SELECT 1 ping with pool cleanup on failure. Updated mock store in MCP tests. 4 new tests.

shots.title: slope store CLI command (status + migrate)
shots.notes: Created src/cli/commands/store.ts following existing CLI patterns. Added getStoreInfo() helper that reads config without opening store (works even when store is broken). Subcommands: status (human + --json), migrate status. Wired into CLI router and registry. 6 tests.

shots.title: Store health check + MCP exposure

hazards.description: Two separate test files (src/mcp/index.test.ts and tests/mcp/index-src.test.ts) both had hardcoded tool count assertions — missed the second file on first pass, caught on test run.

shots.notes: Created src/core/store-health.ts with checkStoreHealth() utility and StoreHealthResult type. Added store_status MCP tool. Updated barrel exports. 5 new health check tests + updated 2 MCP test files for tool count (5→6) and store entries (3→4).

shots.title: Backup/restore + docs

hazards.description: Code review caught WAL checkpoint using readonly DB handle — PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) requires write access. Fixed by removing { readonly: true }.
Code review caught MCP store_status hardcoding 'sqlite' instead of deriving type from config. Fixed by adding storeType parameter to createSlopeToolsServer().
Architect review caught hardcoded migration count '3' and stale JSDoc (5 tools). Fixed with LATEST_SCHEMA_VERSION constant and updated header.

shots.notes: Added backup (WAL checkpoint + copyFileSync for SQLite, pg_dump hint for PG) and restore (validates schema_version before overwriting) subcommands. Created docs/store.md covering config, migrations, CLI, MCP, health check, PG hardening. Both-tier review (code + architect) caught 5 findings — all fixed before merge. 7 new tests.

special_plays: Both-tier review (code + architect) caught 5 findings: 2 critical (WAL checkpoint readonly, MCP hardcoded store type), 3 important (hardcoded migration count, stale JSDoc, unused variable). All fixed before merge.

nutrition.description: Full build + typecheck after every ticket. Full test suite run at each step. 1764 tests passing at completion, 0 failing.
5 commits on feature branch (4 feat + 1 fix for review findings). All pushed before merge. PR #20.
22 new tests across 4 test files (cli/store: 13, core/store-health: 5, store/index: 2, store-pg: 2). Updated 3 existing test files. 1764 total, up from 1706.
Review caught WAL checkpoint on readonly handle — would have caused backup failures in production. Also caught hardcoded store type in MCP that would misreport PG stores as SQLite.

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Clean sprint with predictable scope. Store layer work followed established patterns in both SQLite and PG. The CLI command mirrored session.ts patterns. MCP tool was a thin wrapper over the health check utility. Backup/restore was the most nuanced ticket (WAL checkpoint, validation).
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: Always open SQLite with write access when running PRAGMA wal_checkpoint — readonly handles silently fail or error. When adding MCP tools, check both src/mcp/index.test.ts AND tests/mcp/index-src.test.ts for hardcoded tool counts. Use constants (LATEST_SCHEMA_VERSION) over hardcoded numbers for values derived from array lengths.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: The PG connection string validation with URL parsing caught more edge cases than expected — missing hostname, missing database name, bad prefix all produce clear errors. The compound subquery pattern for getStats() works identically in SQLite and PG with just the project_id WHERE clause difference.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: Store layer is now production-ready with full observability (CLI diagnostics, MCP health checks, backup/restore). S39 can build confidently on this foundation.

bunker_locations: SQLite WAL checkpoint requires write access — never use { readonly: true } before wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)
MCP test files exist in TWO locations (src/mcp/index.test.ts and tests/mcp/index-src.test.ts) — both have hardcoded tool/entry counts
When extending SlopeStore interface, all mock stores in tests must be updated (createMockStore in MCP tests)
PG backup prints pg_dump command with placeholder — cannot use sanitized URL since password masking makes it unusable
yardage_book_updates: src/core/store.ts: StoreStats interface, getSchemaVersion() and getStats() on SlopeStore interface
src/store/index.ts: LATEST_SCHEMA_VERSION export, private getSchemaVersionSync(), async getSchemaVersion(), getStats()
src/store-pg/index.ts: getSchemaVersion(), getStats() with project_id scoping, connection string validation, SELECT 1 ping
src/cli/commands/store.ts: status, migrate status, backup, restore subcommands
src/mcp/index.ts: store_status tool, storeType parameter on createSlopeToolsServer()
docs/store.md: Store configuration, migrations, CLI, MCP, health check, PG hardening docs
course_management_notes: 4 tickets, par 4, score 4 — par. 2 in_the_hole (S38-1, S38-2), 2 green (S38-3 duplicate test miss, S38-4 review findings).
Slope 2 — store layer work with familiar patterns, no new external deps. Matched estimate.
1019 lines added, 37 removed across 19 files. 3 new source files, 3 new test files, 1 new doc file.
Both-tier review (code + architect) caught 5 findings total, all fixed in single review commit before merge.
